features.name: Supabase database schema
features.description: Create V1 tables with RLS on every user-owned table. JWT forwarded from client enforces RLS — service role key never used in user-data routes.

features.name: Auth — Supabase email + password
features.description: Sign-up and sign-in via Supabase Auth (email + password). All app routes are protected — unauthenticated users redirect to /login. JWT forwarded to API routes to instantiate a per-request Supabase client that respects RLS.

features.name: Three-panel app shell
features.description: Fixed three-panel layout: left (AgentPanel), center (WikiPage), right (RightPanel). No collapsing in V1. Each panel manages its own overflow independently.

features.name: AgentPanel component
features.description: Left panel. Message list: user messages right-aligned, assistant left-aligned. Input: multiline textarea, Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline, paperclip icon for file upload. During Phase 1: input disabled, animated indicator with dynamic status label. During Phase 2: text streams token-by-token. KnowledgeUpdateCard appended inline below each assistant message that triggered a wiki mutation.

features.name: WikiPage renderer component
features.description: Center panel. Renders active wiki page top-to-bottom: Title (h1, read-only), Summary, Body (Markdown rendered), Key Points (bulleted), Examples (collapsible, hidden if empty), Related Topics (chip list — each chip loads that page in center), Sources (collapsed by default), Backlinks (collapsed, clickable). Backlinks are computed at read time via SELECT source_page_id FROM page_links WHERE target_page_id = $current — not a stored column. Page navigation via chip/backlink clicks updates active page client-side — no URL-per-page in V1. Empty state: 'Your wiki is empty. Tell me something you learned.'

features.name: RightPanel component
features.description: Right panel. Context-switches automatically based on last AI action — no manual tabs in V1. After page created/updated: related pages (linked titles, clickable). After Q&A: pages referenced in the answer. Default/idle: full page list sorted by last_updated, with search input. New tiles slide up + fade in (8px translate, 200ms ease-out) via Realtime.

features.name: KnowledgeUpdateCard component
features.description: Inline card appended in chat below each assistant message that triggered a wiki mutation. Not dismissible — part of the conversation record. Fields: action badge (Page Created | Page Updated | Pages Linked), affected page name(s) clickable to load in center panel, links added list, one-line summary of what changed. Renders after Supabase Realtime event confirms the DB write.

features.name: Supabase Realtime subscription
features.description: Client subscribes to Realtime on wiki_pages and page_links for the current user. On INSERT/UPDATE: update RightPanel state (triggers slide-in animation), trigger KnowledgeUpdateCard render, refresh WikiPage if the active page was the mutated one. No manual refresh anywhere in the UI.

features.name: Chat API route with Claude tool use
features.description: POST /app/api/chat/route.ts. Uses Vercel AI SDK streamText with all skills bound as tools. Claude is the router — no separate intent classifier. maxSteps: 5 allows tool chaining within a single turn (e.g. create_wiki_page → link_related_pages). If streamText resolves with finishReason === 'max-steps', append a visible notice in chat: 'I ran out of steps — some links may be missing. Continue?' so partial completion is never silent. System prompt injects all page titles + one-line summaries on every request (capped at 50 most recently updated if user has >100 pages). DB writes happen inside tool execute functions only — never in narration text. chat_response streams to client via useChat. Supabase Realtime fires after each confirmed DB write. Tool response contract: every execute function returns { status: 'ok', data: ... } | { status: 'empty' } | { status: 'error', message: string }. System prompt instructs Claude to treat 'empty' as 'not added yet' and 'error' as 'could not reach your wiki' — never conflate the two.

features.name: Tool: create_wiki_page
features.description: Claude tool. Invoked when user provides new knowledge and no page exists for the concept (no exact title or alias match, case-insensitive). Generates structured page JSON, writes to wiki_pages. Chains into link_related_pages automatically via maxSteps.

features.name: Tool: update_wiki_page
features.description: Claude tool. Invoked when user provides knowledge that matches an existing page (exact title or alias match, case-insensitive). Merges new content into existing page fields. Same output schema as create_wiki_page with action: 'update'. Updates wiki_pages row. Chains into link_related_pages for any new related_page_titles.

features.name: Tool: link_related_pages
features.description: Claude tool. Called after create or update. Takes source page id and related_page_titles array. For each title that matches an existing page (exact/alias, case-insensitive), inserts a row into page_links with relationship_type and relationship_reason. Silently skips titles with no matching page. Returns count of links created.

input.relationship_types: { title: string, type: relationship_type_enum, reason: string }[]

features.name: Tool: query_personal_wiki
features.description: Claude tool. Two-step retrieval. Step 1: inject all page titles + summaries; Claude returns ids of relevant pages (up to 10). Step 2: fetch full bodies of selected pages and return them as verbatim excerpts — not summaries. The system prompt instructs Claude to quote from those excerpts directly rather than paraphrase from its own training knowledge. This prevents citation hallucination (attributing to the user's wiki a fact that came from the model). Every answer names the specific pages used inline. If the wiki is insufficient, labels it 'Outside your wiki:' so the user knows what came from their knowledge vs. the model. Returns { status: 'empty' } if no pages match — never conflate with { status: 'error' }.

features.name: Tool: find_knowledge_gaps
features.description: Claude tool. Reviews wiki page coverage for a topic area. Surfaces weakly connected or unresolved areas. Suggests next learning topics or open questions. Returns list of gap descriptions and suggested next steps.

features.name: File upload UI
features.description: Paperclip icon in chat input. Accepts PDF, .txt, .md — max 10 MB. States: idle, uploading (progress indicator in input area), extracting ('Processing your file...' in chat, input disabled), success (normal KnowledgeUpdateCard flow), unsupported type (inline error: 'Unsupported file type. Try PDF, .txt, or .md.'), file too large (inline error: 'File exceeds 10 MB limit.'), parse failure (inline error with suggestion to paste text manually). Wiki is never mutated on any failure.

features.name: File ingestion API route
features.description: POST /app/api/ingest/route.ts. Accepts multipart form data. Validates file type and size before processing. Text extraction: pdf-parse for PDF, native read for .txt/.md. Scanned PDF OCR deferred to V2. On success: passes extracted text to chat route as additional context for create_wiki_page or update_wiki_page. Stores file reference and excerpt in sources table via Supabase Storage.

features.name: Streaming UX — Phase 1 indicator
features.description: During Phase 1 (tool calls in progress): chat input disabled, animated pulsing dots visible, dynamic status label updates per active tool driven by toolInvocations from AI SDK. Label mapping: create_wiki_page → 'Creating page...', update_wiki_page → 'Updating page...', link_related_pages → 'Linking concepts...', query_personal_wiki → 'Searching your wiki...', find_knowledge_gaps → 'Finding gaps...'.

features.name: Streaming UX — wiki section-by-section reveal
features.description: When a page is created or updated, center panel immediately renders a full structured skeleton (CSS pulse animation, sections sized to match expected content). Sections replace skeleton in order as page data arrives via Realtime: Title → Summary → Body → Key Points → Related Topic chips. Each section cross-fades in (150ms ease-out). On update: changed sections flash a brief background highlight to signal what changed.

features.name: Empty state and first-run experience
features.description: New authenticated users see: center panel placeholder ('Your wiki is empty. Tell me something you learned.'), right panel with empty page list and a getting-started prompt, chat seeded with a single companion introduction message. No demo pages pre-populated — the demo always starts from a real empty wiki.

features.name: Deduplication routing
features.description: Before the tool call fires, the API route checks all user page titles and aliases (case-insensitive exact match) against the concept Claude is about to create. If a match is found, routes to update_wiki_page instead of create_wiki_page. No fuzzy or semantic matching in V1 — exact title/alias only.
